lede: A look at October 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Haruka Kudō (singer) and Jon Niese, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Islamic State
events.desc: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant founder and leader Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi kills himself and three children by detonating a suicide vest during the U.S. military Barisha raid in northwestern Syria.

events.title: Leicester City F.C.
events.desc: Leicester City F.C. owner Vichai Srivaddhanaprabha dies in a helicopter crash along with four others after a Premier League match against West Ham United at the King Power Stadium in Leicester, England.

events.title: Widerøe Flight 744
events.desc: Widerøe Flight 744 crashes in Overhalla Municipality, Norway, killing six people.

events.title: Big Bang (financial markets)
events.desc: The British government suddenly deregulates financial markets, leading to a total restructuring of the way in which they operate in the country, in an event now referred to as the Big Bang.

events.title: Ronald Reagan
events.desc: Ronald Reagan delivers a speech on behalf of the Republican candidate for president, Barry Goldwater. The speech launches his political career and comes to be known as "A Time for Choosing".

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German forces capture Banská Bystrica during Slovak National Uprising thus bringing it to an end.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The new British battleship HMS Audacious is sunk by a minefield laid by the armed German merchant-cruiser Berlin.

events.title: Lilburn Boggs
events.desc: Missouri governor Lilburn Boggs issues the Extermination Order, which orders all Mormons to leave the state or be killed.

events.title: George III
events.desc: King George III expands on his Proclamation of Rebellion in the Thirteen Colonies in his speech from the throne at the opening of Parliament.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Ich will den Kreuzstab gerne tragen, BWV 56, one of few works he called a cantata.

deaths.text: Angelo Maria Cicolani, Italian engineer and politician
Xavier Cugat, Spanish-American violinist, bandleader, and actor
